evidence: John Borowski is a master filmmaker of true crime films.
This series by John Borowski just keeps getting better.
Borowski presents Panzram from as many angles as possible.
The film provides an insight into the conditions which created this 'monster', trying to understand his crimes without justifying them.
The movie really needed a musical score to move the narrative forward.
This film steps into the dark world of Vincent Castiglia.
Filmed on a low budget, this film looks like it was filmed for a million bucks.
It is a highly polished independent production that rivals the big studios.
Always a great experience watching any work by filmmaker/author John Borowski.
John Borowski is the master of the true crime documentary film.
The way his artwork was presented and the education of the process was very classy.
This film showed Vincent on the other side of his challenges and the scene where he is sitting on a train and has that smile was the scene that assured me that everything is going to be okay.
I was able to experience a dark fearful emotion that represented Vincent's trials and tribulations.
This film allowed me to experience all those emotions in one.

summary: The user has a strong preference for true crime documentaries, particularly those by John Borowski, appreciating both their emotional depth and production quality. They enjoy complex narratives that evoke a range of emotions, indicating a preference for well-crafted storytelling.
